title: Parenting Skills Classes * Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Parenting Skills Classes * Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions 2 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - Children and Youth With Disabilities/Health Conditions Disabilities Issues Immigrants Refugees/Entrants/Asylees Offender/Ex-Offender Issues Native American Community Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CREATING OPPORTUNITIES REGIONAL FAMILY NETWORK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support services including information and referral, advocacy, and system navigation including Individualized Education Programs (IEPs), Developmental Disabilities (DD) services, healthcare, and other public systems. parenting classes, information and referral, and advocacy services. 680 State Street Suite #100 Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: Children and youth with disabilities and their families Hours: Vary Email (503) 559-0424 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TWO FAMILIES NOW PRACTICE ROUTINES: POSITIVE BEHAVIOR SUPPORT FOR PARENTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Online and offline options available, teaching families of children with special needs and behavioral challenges how to implement effective strategies and routines using a positive behavior support approach. 1203 Willamette Street Suite 100 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (541) 343-4747 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CREATING OPPORTUNITIES REGIONAL FAMILY NETWORK TWO FAMILIES NOW PRACTICE ROUTINES: POSITIVE BEHAVIOR SUPPORT FOR PARENTS
